data_in.description: Differentially accessible peaks (BED format or AnnData object with peak annotations)
Reference genome sequence (for motif scanning context)

data_out.description: Motif enrichment table with columns: motif_id, motif_name, enrichment_score, p_value, and adjusted_p_value

domain_knowledge: Motif enrichment p-values reflect the statistical significance of over- or under-representation of DNA motifs in a peak set compared to a background model or null distribution.
The CIS-BP database is a curated compendium of eukaryotic transcription factor DNA-binding specificity data represented as position-weight matrices.
Differentially accessible peaks are genomic regions with significantly altered chromatin accessibility between cell populations or conditions, typically derived from ATAC-seq or similar assays.
Enrichment scores quantify the degree to which a given motif is overrepresented in peak sequences relative to the background, often computed via hypergeometric or Fisher exact test statistics.
Multiple hypothesis correction (e.g., Benjamini–Hochberg FDR) is typically applied to p-values when testing many motifs simultaneously to control false discovery rate.

cmd: import snapatac2 as snap; import pandas as pd; peaks = snap.read('{inputs.diff_peaks}'); motifs = snap.datasets.cis_bp('{inputs.cis_bp_motifs}'); enrichment = snap.tl.motif_enrichment(peaks, motifs, genome='{inputs.genome_ref}'); enrichment_df = pd.DataFrame(enrichment); enrichment_df.to_csv('motif_enrichment_results.tsv', sep='\t', index=False); print(enrichment_df)

methodology_summary: Load differentially accessible peaks from tl.diff_test output into SnapATAC2 data structure.
Retrieve CIS-BP motif database using datasets.cis_bp, obtaining position-weight matrices and motif identifiers.
Scan peak sequences for motif occurrences using SnapATAC2's integrated motif scanner.
Compute enrichment statistics (scores and p-values) by comparing observed motif frequencies in peaks to background or genome-wide frequencies.
Validation: verify output table contains all required columns (motif_id, enrichment_score, p_value) with no missing values, and that p-values are properly computed statistics between 0 and 1.

task_description: Perform motif enrichment analysis on a set of differentially accessible peaks using SnapATAC2's tl.motif_enrichment function with the CIS-BP motif database, generating an output table with motif identifiers, enrichment scores, and p-values.
task_objective: Invoke tl.motif_enrichment on differentially accessible peaks and verify the output table contains motif IDs, enrichment scores, and p-values.

task_outputs.label: Motif enrichment results with IDs, scores, and p-values

title: Reconstruct motif enrichment analysis using tl.motif_enrichment with the CIS-BP dataset

Synthesis grounding: the following tools/outputs were NOT found in the source paper and are inferred — verify before use: tl.motif_enrichment, tl.diff_test, datasets.cis_bp, Motif enrichment table with columns: motif_id, motif_name, enrichment_score, p_value, and adjusted_p_value.
workflow_description: 1. Load differentially accessible peaks (output from tl.diff_test) as a feature set into SnapATAC2. 2. Load the CIS-BP motif database using datasets.cis_bp to obtain motif definitions and position-weight matrices. 3. Invoke tl.motif_enrichment on the peak set, scanning for motif occurrences within differential regions and computing enrichment statistics against a background model. 4. Retrieve the resulting motif enrichment table containing motif IDs, enrichment scores, and p-values. 5. Validate that all required columns are present and non-null, and that p-values reflect the statistical significance of motif overrepresentation.
